source_notes: Clause semantics map PROCESS constraint-equation activation (icc) and typical reactor-study practice. PROCESS treats selected constraints as equality/inequality equations in the solver; inactive constraints are not enforced. Values below describe the usual DEMO/power-plant configuration, not every optional switch.

authority_behavior.notes: q95 often via fixed/min constraint (e.g. icc related to q95); Greenwald via density-limit constraint equation 5 (i_density_limit models); beta via beta/beta_N limits when activated; net electric and TBR are typical reactor design targets; divertor heat-flux / radiation limits act as proxies for exhaust (not a named detachment solver); coil/stress/fluence inequalities provide lifetime margins; availability enters cost/plant models diagnostically.
